      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "import torch muon_params = []\nadamw_params = []\nfor name, p in model.named_parameters(): if not p.requires_grad: continue is_hidden_2d = p.ndim == 2 and 'embed' not in name and 'lm_head' not in name if is_hidden_2d: muon_params.append(p) else: adamw_params.append(p) optim_muon = torch.optim.Muon(muon_params, lr=0.02, weight_decay=0.1, momentum=0.95, nesterov=True, ns_steps=5, adjust_lr_fn='match_rms_adamw')\noptim_adamw = torch.optim.AdamW(adamw_params, lr=3e-4, weight_decay=0.01) optim_muon.zero_grad()\noptim_adamw.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim_muon.step()\noptim_adamw.step()",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "gram_newton_schulz_symmetric_gemm",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch # Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7): X = X / (X.norm() + eps) wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1] for _ in range(ns_steps): if wide: G = X @ X.T X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X) else: G = X.T @ X X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G) return X momentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",
      "api_symbols": [
        "custom_kernel:gram_newton_schulz",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "import torch # Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7): X = X / (X.norm() + eps) wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1] for _ in range(ns_steps): if wide: G = X @ X.T X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X) else: G = X.T @ X X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G) return X momentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "import torch # Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7): X = X / (X.norm() + eps) wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1] for _ in range(ns_steps): if wide: G = X @ X.T X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X) else: G = X.T @ X X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G) return X momentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

    {
      "feature_id": "tensor_cores",
      "category": "tensor_core",
      "description": "Set torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark=True so that when a cuDNN\nconvolution is called with a new set of size parameters, cuDNN runs\nmultiple algorithms, benchmarks them to find the fastest one, and\nreuses it for the rest of the process for the same size parameters.\nDisabling it (benchmark=False) makes cuDNN deterministically select\nan algorithm, possibly at the cost of reduced performance.       "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",
      "support_level": "supported",

Removes CPU-decode bottleneck for CV/imaging. ~2.18x faster JPEG loading. No-op for text/LLM training.",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch # Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7): X = X / (X.norm() + eps) wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1] for _ in range(ns_steps): if wide: G = X @ X.T X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X) else: G = X.T @ X X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G) return X momentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Cut Cross-Entropy: fuses a linear classifier/logit projection with the cross-entropy loss so the (N x V) logits tensor is never materialized in global memory; chunked online log-sum-exp in SRAM.       "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7): X = X / (X.norm() + eps) wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1] for _ in range(ns_steps): if wide: G = X @ X.T X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X) else: G = X.T @ X X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G) return X momentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch # Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7): X = X / (X.norm() + eps) wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1] for _ in range(ns_steps): if wide: G = X @ X.T X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X) else: G = X.T @ X X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G) return X momentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",
      "support_level": "supported",

Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7): X = X / (X.norm() + eps) wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1] for _ in range(ns_steps): if wide: G = X @ X.T X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X) else: G = X.T @ X X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G) return X momentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "import torch # Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7): X = X / (X.norm() + eps) wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1] for _ in range(ns_steps): if wide: G = X @ X.T X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X) else: G = X.T @ X X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G) return X momentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7): X = X / (X.norm() + eps) wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1] for _ in range(ns_steps): if wide: G = X @ X.T X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X) else: G = X.T @ X X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G) return X momentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch # Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7): X = X / (X.norm() + eps) wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1] for _ in range(ns_steps): if wide: G = X @ X.T X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X) else: G = X.T @ X X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G) return X momentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

Same math (exact CE + gradient), >4x peak-memory cut on large-vocab heads, >2x kernel speed.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom cut_cross_entropy import linear_cross_entropy B, T, D, V = 2, 16, 1024, 32000\nembeddings = torch.randn(B, T, D, device='cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16, requires_grad=True)\nclassifier = torch.randn(V, D, device='cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16, requires_grad=True)\nlabels = torch.randint(0, V, (B, T), device='cuda')\nloss = linear_cross_entropy(embeddings, classifier, labels, shift=1)\nloss.backward()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "cut_cross_entropy.linear_cross_entropy",
        "cut_cross_entropy.transformers.cce_patch"
      ],
      "support_level": "supported",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "limitations": "software technique for cross-entropy over a linear classifier/logit projection; strongest fit is large-vocabulary LM heads, but Apple documents the general logits = X @ A.T + b form; requires compatible PyTorch/Triton/CUDA stack for the Triton CCE path; little benefit when logits materialization is not a memory/time bottleneck; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true"

      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom cut_cross_entropy import linear_cross_entropy B, T, D, V = 2, 16, 1024, 32000\nembeddings = torch.randn(B, T, D, device='cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16, requires_grad=True)\nclassifier = torch.randn(V, D, device='cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16, requires_grad=True)\nlabels = torch.randint(0, V, (B, T), device='cuda')\nloss = linear_cross_entropy(embeddings, classifier, labels, shift=1)\nloss.backward()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "cut_cross_entropy.linear_cross_entropy",
        "cut_cross_entropy.transformers.cce_patch"
      ],
      "support_level": "supported",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "limitations": "software technique for cross-entropy over a linear classifier/logit projection; strongest fit is large-vocabulary LM heads, but Apple documents the general logits = X @ A.T + b form; requires compatible PyTorch/Triton/CUDA stack for the Triton CCE path; little benefit when logits materialization is not a memory/time bottleneck; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true"

      "feature_id": "cut_cross_entropy",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Cut Cross-Entropy: fuses a linear classifier/logit projection with the cross-entropy loss so the (N x V) logits tensor is never materialized in global memory; chunked online log-sum-exp in SRAM. Same math (exact CE + gradient), >4x peak-memory cut on large-vocab heads, >2x kernel speed.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom cut_cross_entropy import linear_cross_entropy B, T, D, V = 2, 16, 1024, 32000\nembeddings = torch.randn(B, T, D, device='cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16, requires_grad=True)\nclassifier = torch.randn(V, D, device='cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16, requires_grad=True)\nlabels = torch.randint(0, V, (B, T), device='cuda')\nloss = linear_cross_entropy(embeddings, classifier, labels, shift=1)\nloss.backward()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "cut_cross_entropy.linear_cross_entropy",
        "cut_cross_entropy.transformers.cce_patch"
      ],
      "support_level": "supported",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "limitations": "software technique for cross-entropy over a linear classifier/logit projection; strongest fit is large-vocabulary LM heads, but Apple documents the general logits = X @ A.T + b form; requires compatible PyTorch/Triton/CUDA stack for the Triton CCE path; little benefit when logits materialization is not a memory/time bottleneck; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true"

      "verified": false,
      "limitations": "software technique for cross-entropy over a linear classifier/logit projection; strongest fit is large-vocabulary LM heads, but Apple documents the general logits = X @ A.T + b form; requires compatible PyTorch/Triton/CUDA stack for the Triton CCE path; little benefit when logits materialization is not a memory/time bottleneck; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true"

      "feature_id": "cut_cross_entropy",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Cut Cross-Entropy: fuses a linear classifier/logit projection with the cross-entropy loss so the (N x V) logits tensor is never materialized in global memory; chunked online log-sum-exp in SRAM. Same math (exact CE + gradient), >4x peak-memory cut on large-vocab heads, >2x kernel speed.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom cut_cross_entropy import linear_cross_entropy B, T, D, V = 2, 16, 1024, 32000\nembeddings = torch.randn(B, T, D, device='cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16, requires_grad=True)\nclassifier = torch.randn(V, D, device='cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16, requires_grad=True)\nlabels = torch.randint(0, V, (B, T), device='cuda')\nloss = linear_cross_entropy(embeddings, classifier, labels, shift=1)\nloss.backward()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "cut_cross_entropy.linear_cross_entropy",
        "cut_cross_entropy.transformers.cce_patch"
      ],

      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "limitations": "software technique for cross-entropy over a linear classifier/logit projection; strongest fit is large-vocabulary LM heads, but Apple documents the general logits = X @ A.T + b form; requires compatible PyTorch/Triton/CUDA stack for the Triton CCE path; little benefit when logits materialization is not a memory/time bottleneck; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true"
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "gram_newton_schulz_symmetric_gemm",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch # Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7): X = X / (X.norm() + eps) wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1] for _ in range(ns_steps): if wide: G = X @ X.T X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X) else: G = X.T @ X X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G) return X momentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",
      "api_symbols": [
        "custom_kernel:gram_newton_schulz",
        "custom_kernel:symmetric_gemm",
        "custom_kernel:triangular_fused_gemm"
      ],
      "usage": "Kernel-level optimization for Muon/Newton-Schulz orthogonalization when profiler evidence shows the NS step is material. The example is a correctness fallback, not the fused production kernel.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "profile Muon optimizer-step time before considering the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel",
        "keep the reference Newton-Schulz implementation for correctness checks and fallback"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no use without Muon/Newton-Schulz workload",
        "no marking verified=true until benchmark and numerical parity are recorded"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "muon_newton_schulz_kernel_candidate",
      "limitations": "custom Gram/Newton-Schulz kernel optimization; recommended=true means candidate only when Muon/Newton-Schulz orthogonalization is material in the step profile, not a generic GEMM replacement; intended for Hopper/Blackwell-class tensor-core kernels; source is primary technical blog, not official vendor doc; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: suggest this only after Muon has been selected and profiler evidence shows orthogonalization dominates; otherwise prefer normal optimizer tuning."

Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7): X = X / (X.norm() + eps) wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1] for _ in range(ns_steps): if wide: G = X @ X.T X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X) else: G = X.T @ X X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G) return X momentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "feature_id": "cut_cross_entropy",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Cut Cross-Entropy: fuses a linear classifier/logit projection with the cross-entropy loss so the (N x V) logits tensor is never materialized in global memory; chunked online log-sum-exp in SRAM. Same math (exact CE + gradient), >4x peak-memory cut on large-vocab heads, >2x kernel speed.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom cut_cross_entropy import linear_cross_entropy B, T, D, V = 2, 16, 1024, 32000\nembeddings = torch.randn(B, T, D, device='cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16, requires_grad=True)\nclassifier = torch.randn(V, D, device='cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16, requires_grad=True)\nlabels = torch.randint(0, V, (B, T), device='cuda')\nloss = linear_cross_entropy(embeddings, classifier, labels, shift=1)\nloss.backward()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "cut_cross_entropy.linear_cross_entropy",
        "cut_cross_entropy.transformers.cce_patch"
      ],
      "support_level": "supported",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "limitations": "software technique for cross-entropy over a linear classifier/logit projection; strongest fit is large-vocabulary LM heads, but Apple documents the general logits = X @ A.T + b form; requires compatible PyTorch/Triton/CUDA stack for the Triton CCE path; little benefit when logits materialization is not a memory/time bottleneck; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true"
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "gram_newton_schulz_symmetric_gemm",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch # Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7): X = X / (X.norm() + eps) wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1] for _ in range(ns_steps): if wide: G = X @ X.T X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X) else: G = X.T @ X X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G) return X momentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",
      "api_symbols": [
        "custom_kernel:gram_newton_schulz",
        "custom_kernel:symmetric_gemm",
        "custom_kernel:triangular_fused_gemm"
      ],
      "usage": "Kernel-level optimization for Muon/Newton-Schulz orthogonalization when profiler evidence shows the NS step is material. The example is a correctness fallback, not the fused production kernel.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "profile Muon optimizer-step time before considering the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel",
        "keep the reference Newton-Schulz implementation for correctness checks and fallback"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no use without Muon/Newton-Schulz workload",
        "no marking verified=true until benchmark and numerical parity are recorded"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "muon_newton_schulz_kernel_candidate",
      "limitations": "custom Gram/Newton-Schulz kernel optimization; recommended=true means candidate only when Muon/Newton-Schulz orthogonalization is material in the step profile, not a generic GEMM replacement; intended for Hopper/Blackwell-class tensor-core kernels; source is primary technical blog, not official vendor doc; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: suggest this only after Muon has been selected and profiler evidence shows orthogonalization dominates; otherwise prefer normal optimizer tuning."

      "feature_id": "cut_cross_entropy",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Cut Cross-Entropy: fuses a linear classifier/logit projection with the cross-entropy loss so the (N x V) logits tensor is never materialized in global memory; chunked online log-sum-exp in SRAM. Same math (exact CE + gradient), >4x peak-memory cut on large-vocab heads, >2x kernel speed.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom cut_cross_entropy import linear_cross_entropy B, T, D, V = 2, 16, 1024, 32000\nembeddings = torch.randn(B, T, D, device='cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16, requires_grad=True)\nclassifier = torch.randn(V, D, device='cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16, requires_grad=True)\nlabels = torch.randint(0, V, (B, T), device='cuda')\nloss = linear_cross_entropy(embeddings, classifier, labels, shift=1)\nloss.backward()",
      "api_symbols": [
        "cut_cross_entropy.linear_cross_entropy",
        "cut_cross_entropy.transformers.cce_patch"
      ],
      "support_level": "supported",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "limitations": "software technique for cross-entropy over a linear classifier/logit projection; strongest fit is large-vocabulary LM heads, but Apple documents the general logits = X @ A.T + b form; requires compatible PyTorch/Triton/CUDA stack for the Triton CCE path; little benefit when logits materialization is not a memory/time bottleneck; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true"
    },
    {
      "feature_id": "gram_newton_schulz_symmetric_gemm",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch # Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7): X = X / (X.norm() + eps) wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1] for _ in range(ns_steps): if wide: G = X @ X.T X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X) else: G = X.T @ X X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G) return X momentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",
      "api_symbols": [
        "custom_kernel:gram_newton_schulz",
        "custom_kernel:symmetric_gemm",
        "custom_kernel:triangular_fused_gemm"
      ],
      "usage": "Kernel-level optimization for Muon/Newton-Schulz orthogonalization when profiler evidence shows the NS step is material. The example is a correctness fallback, not the fused production kernel.",
      "recommended_patterns": [
        "profile Muon optimizer-step time before considering the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel",
        "keep the reference Newton-Schulz implementation for correctness checks and fallback"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no use without Muon/Newton-Schulz workload",
        "no marking verified=true until benchmark and numerical parity are recorded"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "muon_newton_schulz_kernel_candidate",
      "limitations": "custom Gram/Newton-Schulz kernel optimization; recommended=true means candidate only when Muon/Newton-Schulz orthogonalization is material in the step profile, not a generic GEMM replacement; intended for Hopper/Blackwell-class tensor-core kernels; source is primary technical blog, not official vendor doc; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true",
      "notes": "LLM-facing rule: suggest this only after Muon has been selected and profiler evidence shows orthogonalization dominates; otherwise prefer normal optimizer tuning."
    }

      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom cut_cross_entropy import linear_cross_entropy B, T, D, V = 2, 16, 1024, 32000\nembeddings = torch.randn(B, T, D, device='cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16, requires_grad=True)\nclassifier = torch.randn(V, D, device='cuda', dtype=torch.bfloat16, requires_grad=True)\nlabels = torch.randint(0, V, (B, T), device='cuda')\nloss = linear_cross_entropy(embeddings, classifier, labels, shift=1)\nloss.backward()",

    },

      "feature_id": "gram_newton_schulz_symmetric_gemm",
      "category": "kernel_optimization",
      "description": "Gram Newton-Schulz symmetric/triangular fused GEMM: the Newton-Schulz step (used by Muon) is a symmetric product; a triangular fused kernel removes the wasted half of multiplies plus a DRAM round-trip cuBLAS incurs. Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch # Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7): X = X / (X.norm() + eps) wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1] for _ in range(ns_steps): if wide: G = X @ X.T X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X) else: G = X.T @ X X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G) return X momentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",
      "api_symbols": [
        "custom_kernel:gram_newton_schulz",

        "profile Muon optimizer-step time before considering the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel",
        "keep the reference Newton-Schulz implementation for correctness checks and fallback"
      ],
      "avoid_patterns": [
        "no use without Muon/Newton-Schulz workload",
        "no marking verified=true until benchmark and numerical parity are recorded"
      ],
      "support_level": "experimental",
      "recommended": true,
      "verified": false,
      "recommendation_scope": "muon_newton_schulz_kernel_candidate",
      "limitations": "custom Gram/Newton-Schulz kernel optimization; recommended=true means candidate only when Muon/Newton-Schulz orthogonalization is material in the step profile, not a generic GEMM replacement; intended for Hopper/Blackwell-class tensor-core kernels; source is primary technical blog, not official vendor doc; example_code + Nautilus run pending before verified=true",

Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch # Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7): X = X / (X.norm() + eps) wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1] for _ in range(ns_steps): if wide: G = X @ X.T X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X) else: G = X.T @ X X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G) return X momentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",
      "api_symbols": [
        "custom_kernel:gram_newton_schulz",

        "profile Muon optimizer-step time before considering the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel",

Up to 2x faster NS step. Same math (Gram-matrix restart guards stability).",
      "example_code": "import torch # Conceptual fallback only. Production use should call the fused symmetric/triangular GEMM kernel.\ndef gram_newton_schulz_reference(X, ns_steps=5, eps=1e-7): X = X / (X.norm() + eps) wide = X.shape[0] < X.shape[1] for _ in range(ns_steps): if wide: G = X @ X.T X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (G @ X) else: G = X.T @ X X = 1.5 * X - 0.5 * (X @ G) return X momentum_matrix = torch.randn(128, 64)\nupdate = gram_newton_schulz_reference(momentum_matrix)",

      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "description": "nvImageCodec GPU image decode: decodes encoded image bytes (nvJPEG/nvJPEG2000/nvTIFF plus hardware JPEG decode on documented nvJPEG hardware) directly into GPU memory, DLPack zero-copy to torch, decode of batch N+1 overlapping compute of batch N. Removes CPU-decode bottleneck for CV/imaging. ~2.18x faster JPEG loading. No-op for text/LLM training.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom nvidia import nvimgcodec decoder = nvimgcodec.Decoder()\nnv_img = decoder.read('sample.jp2')\ncap = nv_img.to_dlpack()\ntorch_img = torch.from_dlpack(cap)\nassert torch_img.is_cuda\nbatch = torch_img.permute(2, 0, 1).unsqueeze(0).contiguous()",

      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

    {
      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

    {
      "feature_id": "dataset_decomposition",
      "category": "data_pipeline",
      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",
      "support_level": "supported",
      "recommended": true,

      "category": "data_pipeline",

Removes CPU-decode bottleneck for CV/imaging. ~2.18x faster JPEG loading. No-op for text/LLM training.",
      "example_code": "import torch\nfrom nvidia import nvimgcodec decoder = nvimgcodec.Decoder()\nnv_img = decoder.read('sample.jp2')\ncap = nv_img.to_dlpack()\ntorch_img = torch.from_dlpack(cap)\nassert torch_img.is_cuda\nbatch = torch_img.permute(2, 0, 1).unsqueeze(0).contiguous()",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "description": "Muon optimizer: for 2D weight matrices, replaces the momentum-smoothed gradient with its nearest semi-orthogonal matrix via a fixed 5-step Newton-Schulz iteration (no SVD); 1D params stay on AdamW. Matches AdamW final loss using ~52% of training FLOPs.",
      "example_code": "import torch muon_params = []\nadamw_params = []\nfor name, p in model.named_parameters(): if not p.requires_grad: continue is_hidden_2d = p.ndim == 2 and 'embed' not in name and 'lm_head' not in name if is_hidden_2d: muon_params.append(p) else: adamw_params.append(p) optim_muon = torch.optim.Muon(muon_params, lr=0.02, weight_decay=0.1, momentum=0.95, nesterov=True, ns_steps=5, adjust_lr_fn='match_rms_adamw')\noptim_adamw = torch.optim.AdamW(adamw_params, lr=3e-4, weight_decay=0.01) optim_muon.zero_grad()\noptim_adamw.zero_grad()\nloss.backward()\noptim_muon.step()\noptim_adamw.step()",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",

      "description": "Dataset Decomposition: decompose corpus into same-length buckets and train a short-to-long variable-sequence-length curriculum with only intra-document attention. Changes data ordering not model/loss; reaches target accuracy up to 6x faster than fixed-length concatenation.",
      "example_code": "def decompose_documents(tokenized_docs, bucket_lengths=(128, 256, 512, 1024, 2048)): buckets = {L: [] for L in bucket_lengths} for doc in tokenized_docs: n = len(doc) for L in bucket_lengths: for start in range(0, n - L + 1, L): # never concatenate across documents buckets[L].append(doc[start:start + L]) return buckets def sample_curriculum_bucket(buckets, step, total_steps): lengths = sorted([L for L, seqs in buckets.items() if seqs]) max_idx = max(0, int((len(lengths) - 1) * step / max(1, total_steps - 1))) active_lengths = lengths[:max_idx + 1] return active_lengths[-1]",
